compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: First review BLOCK remediation: accepted fe8099b669b73f3b3/f02ec8e5e930a1f9d by destructuring threshold_for into (block_threshold, blocking_enabled) and flooring only the float through an explicit pass3_over_findings execution_review wrapper. Accepted febe10b4ccf0d00da/ff8eaeafcec43dd7e with concrete unit and production integration commands. Accepted fd3563a76a5585c13/f43f9ecc654a77dfd with the exact wrapper injection seam and manifest token grammar. Added explicit validity consumers, re-sign phase persistence, fixed-order manifest restructuring, stable-vs-unstable transaction outcomes, LLM no-write behavior, compaction/cache semantics, logging, rollback, and advanced documentation. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Second review BLOCK remediation: accepted f502632be5bbe2064/fa387e521751190d4 by adding a measurable advanced-doc AC and docs test. Accepted f04b77e5bbf376c71 by naming manifest.validate_review_phase_metadata plus sidecar.parse_review_phase_metadata and their exact errors. Accepted f5f565f059103b643 by defining lowercase string/JSON number/omitted-key v2 serialization and every null/type/combination failure. Accepted workflow advisories by threading review_phase through precheck output, gate YAML, decide input, and the execution_review closure, plus a production execution-floor integration. Corrected 75de prerequisite wording, split exact rollback preservation from documented precision loss, explained signed-vs-compiled phase roles, and fixed all retries to the immutable pre-LLM baseline. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Third review BLOCK remediation: accepted fc747c1f359419f9c/f2f859a16ae49197b by naming and wiring both plan-review.yaml decide blocks, requiring review_phase in both closed schemas, and adding exact branch coverage. Accepted the call-site advisory by also passing precheck review_phase through gate_dispatch verify-failure recovery, with legacy absence defaulting planning; all other intentional planning callers use the false default. Added version-coherent schema/YAML/code rollout and golden/cutover coverage. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fourth review BLOCK remediation: accepted fc9c130d2875d37bc by correcting schema state: precheck output remains closed, decide input remains intentionally open, and both declare/require review_phase atomically. Golden tests assert those distinct additionalProperties postures plus version-coherent rollback. Also accepted the rollback advisory by requiring compact to pause for a lossless downgrade window while retaining the documented bootstrap recovery if operators do not pause it. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Certified PASS advisory disposition: f96e73233ac9a9a4d accepted as an operational rollback invariant—pause re-sign as well as compact for the downgrade window because old v2 readers ignore additive phase fields; document this with the other rollback steps. fbdeb765231828bec is already covered by the explicitly named tests/unit/test_plan_review_phase_reducer.py command, whose description includes rollback precision behavior; no extra file is needed. fd81c8cf85201e941 is inapplicable because plan reviews are synchronous and there is no cross-version in-flight workflow payload; gate_dispatch legacy absence handling is defense-in-depth. fb59727dfac76fbbf/f607dd457c67f9209 are non-blocking size/style diagnostics for a cohesive phase-plus-atomic-signing protocol. fb687356588601a04 is deliberately resolved by the accepted documented bootstrap precision loss rather than adding fsck scope. f009ca35a68ba943c is declined: the user explicitly approved a fixed non-configurable 0.80 policy with code rollback as removal mechanism. f77836e85c31843cd maps relationship and related-fingerprint directly to PlanRelationSnapshot relationship sets and related_material fields. f686d2afb53de54e6 is declined because the named immutable generation type makes equality and retry invariants reviewable across all three signing paths. fa50cb802e329b7b7 accepted for implementation: define one EXECUTION_REVIEW_PRIORITY_FLOOR=0.80 protocol constant and use it for manifest, Pass-3, sidecar, and validity comparisons. No signed material change is required.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implementation and local verification complete. TDD evidence: 46/46 intended behavior cases observed RED before implementation; implementer received only the happy path; restored held-out oracle passed; defect-seeded removal of the 0.80 floor correctly failed. Final frozen tree: make check PASS; make test 6883 passed, 28 skipped, 112 deselected; parallel diagnostic 6878 passed, 28 skipped; diff check clean; hard-cap modules signing.py 798, attest.py 800. Acceptance audit added real lifecycle proofs for signing and workflow recovery, documentation headings, snapshot pass-through, and compaction preservation. Ready for Gerrit review.

compiled_state.description:
# Add execution-phase plan reviews

## Goal
Allow plan changes made during implementation to be reviewed with a fixed 0.80 blocking floor, while preserving stricter planning reviews and preventing a signature from binding phase or related material that changed during review.

## Implementation
- Add reducer-maintained `plan_review_phase: planning|execution` to compiled state, distinct from prompt `execution_mode`. Only the winning/normal `process_status` branch updates it: a committed winning STATUS target `in_progress` sets execution; target `open` sets planning; every other target preserves the current value. Losing fork events never update it, and force transitions use the same winning target rule. Carry the field through SNAPSHOT compaction. Verify `_snapshot_strip_keys()` in `_commands/compact.py` does not strip it. Review reads the compiled field O(1), never scans raw history.
- For a pre-feature snapshot without the field, bootstrap planning when compiled status is `open` or `idea`, execution for every other known status. An unknown status raises the existing reducer validation error rather than guessing. Bump `reducer/_cache.py::_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` so the first post-upgrade reduction applies this once; later snapshots persist it. Emit one structured INFO `plan_review_phase_bootstrap` with ticket ID, compiled status, and chosen phase. This is a cache recomputation, not a write/compaction wave: ordinary reads rebuild the local derived cache lazily and do not append ticket events.
- Restructure `build_manifest`'s fixed initial list to emit exact lines in this order after `advisory:` and before `rebar-version:`: `review-phase: planning|execution`, then for execution only `priority-floor: 0.80`. Each line has exactly a prefix, one ASCII space, and one token. Add `manifest.validate_review_phase_metadata(phase: object, floor: object, *, legacy_absent: bool) -> ReviewPhaseMetadata` as the single grammar/policy helper; `ReviewPhaseMetadata` is exactly `{phase: planning|execution, priority_floor: float|None}`. Manifest parsers extract raw tokens and call it. `manifest_review_phase()` returns legacy planning only when the line is absent; `manifest_priority_floor()` returns `None` only when absent. The helper raises `ManifestFormatError` for duplicates signaled by callers, missing/extra tokens, unknown phase, bool/non-numeric/non-finite floor, or floor outside `[0.0, 1.0]`; a floor without a phase and execution without exactly one floor are malformed. Existing parsers scan by prefix rather than fixed offsets, so added lines do not change regver/material/dependency parsing. The signed line records the phase the LLM ran under; compiled state records current phase, and compatibility compares these distinct values rather than comparing current state to itself.
- Extend 75de's `DerivedPlanReviewHealth` with always-present `phase_status: compatible|incompatible|malformed`. Compatibility table: current planning + signed planning/legacy planning = compatible; current planning + signed execution = incompatible; current execution + signed planning/legacy planning = compatible; current execution + signed execution floor >= 0.80 = compatible; any malformed phase/floor or execution floor < 0.80 = malformed/incompatible respectively (`malformed` is syntax/shape only, `incompatible` is syntactically valid policy mismatch). The fixed 0.80 protocol policy is not configurable. Raising it later invalidates lower-floor execution attestations; lowering it accepts higher-floor attestations without migration.
- Extend 75de's `compute_validity` plan-review branch to parse phase/floor with pins after signature/kind verification and return phase_status on every later result. After prerequisite 75de is implemented on the feature branch, claim, idempotent reuse, and drift refresh share `compute_validity(DEFAULT|DRIFT_REFRESH)` and inherit phase enforcement. Child 2bbd's later CLOSE call inherits it. Child cb6b's later audit/UI health reads the same result. Completion-verifier calls never invoke phase, floor, or pin parsers.
- Persist review-time phase data for failed-sign recovery with an exact additive `plan_review_result_v2` schema: new full reviews always write JSON `review_phase` as lowercase string `planning` or `execution`; planning omits `priority_floor`; execution writes JSON number `priority_floor: 0.8` (not string and not null). For legacy sidecars, both keys absent means planning/None. A present null, only-one-key legacy combination, bool, string floor, unknown phase, planning with a floor, or execution without a floor is malformed. Add `sidecar.parse_review_phase_metadata(payload) -> ReviewPhaseMetadata`; it passes raw JSON values to `manifest.validate_review_phase_metadata` and translates `ManifestFormatError` to `SidecarReviewPhaseError`. `resign_plan_review` calls this named parser, compares the result with current compiled phase, and refuses incompatible/malformed re-sign. A passing re-sign builds the manifest from the recorded review-time phase/floor, not a newly inferred phase. This edit composes after 75de's related-material sidecar guard and before acaf's current pin collection/sign step.
- Thread phase through every Pass-3 entry. Add required `review_phase` with string enum `planning|execution` to both schemas atomically: update the already-closed `plan_review_precheck_output.schema.json` properties/required arrays while retaining `additionalProperties: false`; update the intentionally open `plan_review_decide_input.schema.json` properties/required arrays while retaining `additionalProperties: true`. `workflow_ops.plan_review_precheck` emits the compiled value. In `llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml`, add `review_phase: ${{ steps.precheck.outputs.review_phase }}` to both `plan_review_decide` `with:` blocks: the code-grounded verify branch and the non-agentic else branch. `plan_review_decide` requires the field and calls `orchestrator.pass3_over_findings(..., execution_review=(review_phase == "execution"))`. The verify-failure recovery in `workflow/gate_dispatch.py` reads `precheck["review_phase"]` and passes the same bool; absence in a legacy persisted run defaults planning. These are the complete call sites: normal workflow calls pass the explicit bool; direct unit/legacy callers may use the default false only when they intentionally model planning. The schema/YAML/code ship atomically from one checkout; workflows are synchronous and have no cross-version in-flight server state. Update schema golden/cutover tests for the closed output contract and rollback together.
- Define `orchestrator.pass3_over_findings(findings, verifs, *, execution_review: bool = False)` exactly at the module-level function. Its local `_threshold_for` closure lexically captures the parameter, calls `_criteria.threshold_for(...)`, destructures `(block_threshold, blocking_enabled)`, and returns `(max(block_threshold, 0.80), blocking_enabled)` only when `execution_review and blocking_enabled`; otherwise it returns the original pair. Pass this closure to `review_kernel.pass3_over_findings`. This changes normal Pass-3 only: advisory-only criteria keep `blocking_enabled=False`, novelty and completion floors are untouched, deterministic structural failures remain blocking, and sub-floor findings remain visible.
- Own one pre-sign generation transaction for ordinary review, drift refresh, and re-sign. `PlanReviewGeneration` is `{phase, priority_floor, own_material, relation_snapshot, ticket_store_revision}` where `relation_snapshot` is child acaf's exact `PlanRelationSnapshot` and equality is dataclass equality across every field. Full review collects the initial generation before the LLM; drift refresh and re-sign collect it immediately before their non-LLM probe. No ticket-store write occurs before the model/probe succeeds, so an LLM/provider failure retains existing unsigned INDETERMINATE behavior and leaves no partial state.
- Before signing, retain one immutable `initial_generation` captured before the LLM/probe; retries never replace this baseline. Retry outside the lock at most three times only for an unstable tracker revision: read clean tracker HEAD, collect a fresh generation, read HEAD again; unequal HEADs emit structured WARN `plan_review_generation_retry` with attempt/before/after and retry. A stable fresh generation unequal to the initial generation is terminal unsigned stale (`plan_review_generation_changed`) and requires re-review; it is not retried. Under the global write lock, require the same clean HEAD and exact generation, then append and commit the signature atomically. Under-lock HEAD/generation mismatch consumes a retry; exhaustion returns retryable unsigned. `LockTimeout` returns retryable unsigned without writing. Dirty/unmerged tracker or other terminal collection/sign failure emits structured ERROR `plan_review_sign_aborted` and returns unsigned without traceback.
- Document the phase/floor manifest contract, health compatibility table, fixed-floor rationale, generation transaction, and rollback behavior in advanced `docs/plan-review-gate.md`. Rollback disables claim/close enforcement first and pauses every `rebar compact` invocation for the downgrade window; with compaction paused, existing phase-bearing snapshots remain lossless. Old reducers preserve the unknown snapshot field when replaying an existing snapshot. If old code fully re-reduces raw events after its own cache bump, the phase field is absent and a later forward upgrade reboots it from compiled status; operators accept that documented loss of historical phase precision during the rollback window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Winning entry into `in_progress` sets compiled `plan_review_phase=execution`.
- [ ] Winning entry into `open` sets compiled `plan_review_phase=planning`.
- [ ] Other winning status targets preserve the projection.
- [ ] Losing fork STATUS events never update the projection.
- [ ] Force transitions follow the same winning target rule.
- [ ] Normal SNAPSHOT compaction and older-reducer snapshot pass-through preserve the exact field value.
- [ ] The rollback test proves old-code raw-event re-reduction drops the unknown field and forward upgrade reboots it from compiled status, with the documented precision-loss window.
- [ ] Pre-feature open/idea snapshots bootstrap planning once; every other known status bootstraps execution once.
- [ ] Bootstrap emits the exact structured INFO and does not append ticket events.
- [ ] Reducer-cache versioning activates the lazy bootstrap idempotently.
- [ ] Planning manifests contain exactly `review-phase: planning` in the documented position.
- [ ] Execution manifests contain exactly `review-phase: execution` then `priority-floor: 0.80` in the documented position.
- [ ] Legacy phase-less attestations parse as planning.
- [ ] Every named phase/floor syntax error raises `ManifestFormatError`; existing manifest parsers remain unchanged.
- [ ] Planning state accepts planning/legacy and rejects syntactically valid execution attestations.
- [ ] Execution state accepts planning/legacy and execution floor >= 0.80.
- [ ] Execution state rejects a syntactically valid execution floor < 0.80 as incompatible.
- [ ] Malformed phase/floor produces `phase_status=malformed`.
- [ ] `DerivedPlanReviewHealth.phase_status` is present on every plan-review validity result.
- [ ] Claim, reuse, drift refresh, and re-sign enforce phase through the shared validity/phase helpers; child 2bbd and cb6b consume the same boundary later.
- [ ] Completion-verifier validity never calls phase, floor, or pin parsers.
- [ ] Re-sign parses the exact v2 JSON phase fields through `sidecar.parse_review_phase_metadata`, uses the recorded values, and refuses every incompatible/malformed variant.
- [ ] Execution Pass-3 destructures `(block_threshold, blocking_enabled)` and floors only the float threshold when blocking is enabled.
- [ ] A production workflow integration test starts from compiled execution phase and proves both YAML decide branches receive `review_phase` and block a finding below 0.80 that planning would not block.
- [ ] The verify-failure recovery test proves `gate_dispatch` passes execution phase to Pass-3 and legacy missing phase defaults planning.
- [ ] Schema golden tests prove `review_phase` is required in both schemas, precheck remains closed, decide input remains open, and schema/YAML/code rollback is version-coherent.
- [ ] Planning Pass-3 returns the original threshold pair unchanged.
- [ ] Advisory-only criteria, novelty/completion floors, deterministic failures, and visible sub-floor findings retain their documented behavior.
- [ ] A stable generation equal to the initial generation signs and commits under one lock.
- [ ] Any stable phase, own-material, relationship, related-fingerprint, or generation-field change returns terminal unsigned stale.
- [ ] HEAD instability retries at most three times with exact WARN fields, then returns retryable unsigned.
- [ ] Under-lock mismatch retries; `LockTimeout` returns retryable unsigned; neither writes a signature.
- [ ] Dirty/unmerged or terminal signing failure returns unsigned and emits `plan_review_sign_aborted`.
- [ ] Model/provider failure before signing writes no ticket event or signature and retains existing unsigned INDETERMINATE behavior.
- [ ] The production-path integration test covers stable collection, under-lock HEAD re-verification, signature append, and commit end to end.
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` contains sections for the phase/floor manifest contract, phase_status compatibility table, fixed-0.80 rationale, PlanReviewGeneration transaction, and rollback precision-loss behavior.

## Verification
- `pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_phase_reducer.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_phase_manifest.py` proves lifecycle projection, bootstrap, compaction, grammar, ordering, and parser compatibility.
- `pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_phase_validity.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_execution_floor.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_resign.py` proves the compatibility table, every consumer seam, tuple-aware threshold wrapping, completion isolation, and sidecar recovery.
- `pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_sign_transaction.py` proves stable/change/retry/exhaustion/dirty/LockTimeout/logging and no-write failure paths.
- `pytest -q tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_sign_transaction.py tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_execution_floor.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_workflow_schema_golden.py` runs production signing, both automatic execution-floor branches, verify-failure recovery, and closed-schema contract checks.
- `pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_docs.py` verifies the five required advanced-documentation sections.
- Run `make check` and the full non-LLM test suite, including the named integration and rollback tests.

file_impact.reason: Maintain winning-branch plan_review_phase projection.
Bump reducer cache for lazy phase bootstrap.
Verify compaction preserves the phase field.
Encode, parse, and validate exact phase/floor metadata.
Carry review generation and wrap Pass-3 threshold pairs.
Emit and consume compiled review phase in workflow steps.
Thread review_phase through both decide branches.
Preserve execution phase in verify-failure Pass-3 recovery.
Expose required review_phase from precheck.
Require review_phase at decision.
Enforce phase compatibility and unified pre-sign generation checks.
Recover using parsed sidecar-recorded review phase and floor.
Persist and parse exact additive v2 phase fields.
Document advanced phase, floor, transaction, and rollback contracts.
Prove lifecycle projection, bootstrap, compaction, and rollback precision behavior.
Prove manifest and sidecar grammar, ordering, and parser compatibility.
Prove phase compatibility and shared consumer behavior.
Prove tuple-aware execution threshold wrapping.
Prove exact sidecar phase recovery.
Prove immutable-baseline comparison, retries, locks, logs, and failure paths.
Verify all required advanced documentation sections.
Exercise production signature append and commit under lock.
Exercise both automatic execution-floor branches and verify-failure recovery.
Verify closed schema and workflow wiring contracts.

compiled_state.title: Add planning and execution phases to plan-review attestations
